events.title: 🌈 GSOPride Drag Brunch
events.description:
It’s official, your Miss Greensboro Pride 2025 is Stormy StJames.
Welcome to the Royal Court, Stormy, alongside Stella Fahrenheit Reddy (Mr Greensboro Pride), Chantelle Alizé Courvoisier (Mx Greensboro Pride), and Paisley Parque (Miss Greensboro Pride Trans). We couldn’t be more thrilled.
Come crown her with us on Saturday, June 20, at our GSOPride Drag Brunch at Oden Brewing Company, headlined by RPDR’s India Ferrah. Doors at noon, show through 2:30. Ticket link in the comments.
Welcome to the reign, Stormy.
IMPAQT GSO #impaqtgso #greensboropride #pride

events.title: 🌈 Ice Breaker Board Games
events.location: Dragon’s Hoard, 4645 W Market St Suite A, Greensboro, NC
events.description:
🎲 Tomorrow night, your fate awaits at Gaymers Guild! 🌙

Join IMPAQT GSO & Allies & Outlaws at Dragon’s Hoard for a night of games, deception, strategy, and new friendships. Whether you’re a board game veteran or curious about trying something new, there’s a seat for you at the table.

🧩 Ice Breaker Board Games — 5:30PM–7PM
🕰️ Blood on the Clocktower — 7PM onwards
📍 4645 W Market St Suite A, Greensboro, NC
📌 Find us in the back lounge area near the couch & TV setup inside Dragon’s Hoard.

Expect bluffing, alliances, dramatic accusations, chaotic laughs, and maybe a little betrayal before the night is over 👀

✨ Come solo, bring friends, and make new ones along the way!

#GaymersGuild #IMPAQTGSO

events.description:

Guilford Courthouse National Military Park Visitor Center
2332 New Garden Rd, Greensboro

events.title: 🌈 SIX The Musical - Opening Night
events.location: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
events.description:
It’s time to take back the microphone and reclaim your crown!

We are SO excited to give away 4 tickets to the global sensation SIX for the opening night performance on Tuesday, May 5th, at the Tanger Center!

From Tudor Queens to Pop Icons, the SIX wives of Henry VIII remix five hundred years of historical heartbreak into a euphoric celebration of 21st-century power. The New York Times says SIX “TOTALLY RULES!” and The Washington Post hails it as “exactly the kind of energizing, inspirational illumination this town aches for!"

To enter: Tell us about a moment you felt fierce, stood up for yourself, or took back your narrative. We want to hear those "reclaim your crown" moments!

Two winners will be chosen to snag 2 tickets each for the May 5th show.

Show Details: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts Tuesday, May 5th (Show runs May 5-10).

 If you can't make the opening night, you can get tickets from the link in the comments.

We can’t wait to read your stories below. Let’s show them how Greensboro shines!

#SIXTheMusical #GreensboroPride #IMPAQTGSO #TangerCenter #ReclaimYourCrown

events.title: 🌈 Lesbian Visibility Day
events.description:
🧡🤍💖 Today we celebrate Lesbian Visibility Day!!!🎉🎉🎉

A moment to honor the strength, love, resilience, and contributions of lesbian women everywhere. Visibility isn’t just about being seen, it’s about being recognized, valued, and uplifted in every space we enter.

As trailblazer Sally Ride once said:
“You can’t be what you can’t see.”

That truth still resonates. Visibility creates possibility. It opens doors for the next generation to live boldly, authentically, and without apology.

Today, we celebrate:
🧡 The stories that have been told, and the ones still waiting to be heard
🤍 The courage it takes to live openly and honestly
💖 The community that continues to build spaces of love, safety, and empowerment

To every lesbian who has ever questioned if they belong, you do. Your existence matters. Your love matters. Your visibility matters.

Let’s continue to show up, speak out, and stand proud, not just today, but every day.

#LesbianVisibilityDay #VisibilityMatters #Pride365 #GSOPride

events.title: 🎨 Public Open Studio Hours
events.description:
MEET MACY ✨ Current Artist in Residence, as part of the GPS Student Residency Program | Summer Studios: Arts on Site! ✨

About Macy macyjart 
Macy James (BFA Sculpture & Ceramics) will explore various materials that she has yet to try, though has long wanted to. Within this residency, Macy plans to work with more accessible material specifically, in addition to those she is accustomed to, like found and second-hand goods sourced from local places - materials that do not require large shops or expensive tools. Another material interest is found wood, like driftwood, to experiment with wood carving or wood burning alongside bead and wire sculptures, which she presently has no experience working with. The hope is that this will help her develop a healthy habit of creating art with widely accessible materials and make her an overall more multifaceted artist.

About the Student Residency Program:
Designed to support UNCG undergraduate student artists (BA + BFA) striving to develop, adapt, and/or reinvent their creative process and to promote artistic growth and development, artists are afforded two-weeks of uninterrupted research and development, coupled with financial support and public presentational platforms to share their creative work. 

Join our AIRs in their studio practices 📅:
May 11-22, 2026

Public Open Studio Hours:
Monday, May 18th | 12-3P
Wednesday, May 20th | 2-5P

Closing Reception + Open Studio: 
Friday, May 22nd | 6-8P

Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile 

uncgarts uncgartschool cvpastudentsuccess

events.title: 🎨 4th Annual BA Student Exhibition
events.description:
A closer look into the 4th Annual BA Student Exhibition ✨🎨

Shoutout to our amazing BA student artists and the interns who helped curate this show.

*********************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
4th Annual BA Student Exhibition
Curated by GPS Interns:
Naomi Chavez, Kate Currivan, Melanie St. Clair
Exhibition: May 5-9, 2026
Reception: Thursday, May 7th, 6-8p
Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Thursday 12-5p
Friday, CLOSED Saturday, 2-5p
This exhibition marks the fourth annual Bachelor of Arts student exhibition hosted by Greensboro Project Space. Students are given the opportunity to display the skills that they have built during their time as an undergraduate student at UNC Greensboro.
Greensboro Project Space is proud to showcase BA students’ ambition, drive, and creative power to our community. These students have formed their own journey on their own terms; exploring painting, drawing, printmaking, sculpture, animation, and new media. We invite you to experience and celebrate their creative development throughout their academic journey.
Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile
uncg uncgarts uncgartschool cvpastudents

events.title: 🎨 Homebody
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us for the reception of Homebody by Kathryn Hood!🥂

The exhibition is still open until 5pm — don’t miss out on experiencing Homebody

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Homebody

MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Kathryn Hood

Exhibition: April 28 - May 2, 2026
Reception: Friday, May 1st, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Homebody is an exhibition of collages and sculptures in which Kathryn Hood carves her perceptions of tense muscles into paper and upholstery foam. She embraces the body-mind connection, an essential tool for self-understanding, as she houses ungrounded avatars within handmade paper and muscular furnishings outside of formal traditions. Hood composes the interior feelings of ideas & emotions with the exterior feeling of tension. This duality of perception reflects the disparate nature of themes explored, such as femininity, domesticity, and the performance of identity.

Homebody is the residence of textured perceptions. This space is an act of resensitization.

Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: 🎨 Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us in celebrating the reception Garden of Corporeal Fantasy by Victoria Mercado-Lues

The exhibition is still open until 5pm — step inside before it closes

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Victoria Mercado-Lues

Exhibition: April 21 - 25, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 24th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Brightly saturated and fantastical, Garden of Corporeal Fantasy explores the meeting of nature and flesh in oil paintings and ceramics. With invented creatures and visceral biological imagery, the works reframe the gaze towards the bodily through a lens of wonder — a less punitive lens than the centuries-long and still recurring traditions of bodies as commodified, objectified, or sinful.

This exhibition addresses our discomfort surrounding the body by combining it with the godly imposing presence of the landscape, like waterfalls and billowing clouds. In this combination, the work is imbued with a devotional, almost sacred quality, borrowing visuals of the holy and filling it with the biological. The sticky, hairy, fleshy becomes something worth staring at with open fascination, the same way we are in awe of exotic plants, or deep-sea creatures, illuminating the sublime inherent in the corporeal.
Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

events.description:
The BLACK CAT is back JUNE 13th!! 🐈‍⬛

Celebrating two years of communing in the dark, dancing, screaming, and celebrating our shared weirdness. My love for this community and scene are beyond words 🖤🦇

etc.gso 
1333 Grove St. GSO

Music 9pm
$13 NOTAFLOF
All ages, BYOB for folks 21+ (with ID)

events.title: 🎨 Primal Static, Linechalk, Crushing Quiet - Live
events.description:
Friday, May 22nd doors at 8 // show at 9
$10 suggested 

Hosted by blueorpheuslive with performances by: primalstaticmusic linechalkofficial & crushingquietband 

Primal Static transports the soul of blues-rock and ferociously cast it into an array of songs filled with dizzying riffs and powerful lyrics. Something about Primal Static’s music sinks its teeth into you. Fearless, but also gentle in their attack. Truly one of a kind.

Linechalk describe themselves as “rock with no rules” hailing from Winston-Salem. 

Crushing Quiet is an indie rock band based in Greensboro.

events.title: 🎨 Death Circle
events.description:
𝔇𝔢𝔞𝔱𝔥 ℭ𝔦𝔯𝔠𝔩𝔢, 𝔱𝔬𝔡𝔞𝔶 𝔣𝔯𝔬𝔪 5 𝔱𝔬 7 𝔓𝔐 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs 🖤

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We’ll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there — 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: 🎨 Advance Base & Moontype with Likewindowpanes
events.description:
advance_base & moontypo are coming back to Greensboro with local support from likewindowpanes on Wednesday, February 25th 👾🎶🎹

Doors at 7pm and music at 8pm // $12 suggested 

This is going to be a really sweet show. We’re super thrilled to have these folks back in Greensboro. If you missed Advance Base for his winter tour in 2024 or Moontype last summer, this is your chance to see them both with Greensboro’s own, Panes. 

Advance Base is the melancholic soft rock recording project of Chicago, IL singer/songwriter Owen Ashworth (formerly of Casiotone for the Painfully Alone). Using a two-handed arsenal of electric piano, Omnichord, samplers, effect pedals & drum machines, Ashworth builds minimalist, heavy-hearted, nostalgia-obsessed ballads around his conversational baritone. The warm, electronic sound of Advance Base has been described as “lo-fi,” “depressed” & “weirdly uplifting.” Advance Base released Horrible Occurrences in 2024.

Moontype is songwriter Margaret McCarthy’s dreamworld come to life. Based in Chicago, Illinois, the band features Emerson Hunton on drums, and Joe Suihkonen and Andrew Clinkman on guitar. Quiet emotions that McCarthy explores with her bass become, with the band, as large and as musically complex as a mood itself: cathartic, angsty, and unmet. Moontype released their debut album, Bodies of Water, in 2021 to critical acclaim.

panes is minimal introspective music with an engaging, dreamy listlessness. ever careening toward some kind of undiscovered self epiphany.

Flyer by: likewindowpanes

events.title: 🎨 Night of Cosmic Sound Exploration
events.description:
𝗝𝗼𝗶𝗻 𝘂𝘀 𝗼𝗻 𝗧𝗵𝘂𝗿𝘀𝗱𝗮𝘆, 𝗙𝗲𝗯𝗿𝘂𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝟭𝟵𝘁𝗵, 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮 𝗻𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 𝗼𝗳 𝗰𝗼𝘀𝗺𝗶𝗰 𝘀𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗹𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 🔊🪐🎵🐴

𝙈𝙖𝙥𝙨 features Brian Haran (Ama Divers, Filthybirds; engineer on numerous records) handling electronics/oscillators/tapeloops, Nick Vanbuskirk on sax (Panes, Kill the Buddha, bedroom division, and countless others), along with Tyler Monroe behind the drums (plays with too many people to count). This project is 100% improv—noisy ambient colliding with avant jazz. brian.s.haran likewindowpanes _tyler.monroe_ 

𝙇𝙪𝙠𝙚 𝙎𝙘𝙝𝙣𝙚𝙞𝙙𝙚𝙧 is a Nashville based pedal steel guitarist who has worked with a variety of artists including Margo Price, Orville Peck, S.G. Goodman, William Tyler, and Futurebirds. His debut solo album of ambient New Age music created entirely on pedal steel, ‘Altar of Harmony,’ was released by Third Man Records in 2020. His new EP ‘For Dancing In Quiet Light’ is out now via Leaving Records. lukefromnashville 

𝙎𝙖𝙖𝙥𝙖𝙩𝙤 is the music project of upstate New York–based sound artist Brendan Principato, who weaves manipulated field recordings with lush electronic soundscapes exploring ecology and music. He has held residencies with the National and Alaska State Park Services, including 2023 near Juneau and 2022 on Fire Island. His recordings have been named Best Ambient on Bandcamp, and his live performances include collaborations with Laraaji, Don Slepian, Patricia Wolf, and Green-House. saapato

events.title: 🎨 Death Circle
events.description:
𝔇𝔢𝔞𝔱𝔥 ℭ𝔦𝔯𝔠𝔩𝔢, 𝔱𝔬𝔡𝔞𝔶 𝔣𝔯𝔬𝔪 5 𝔱𝔬 7 𝔓𝔐 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs 🖤

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We’ll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there — 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: 🎨 Show with jacobcmckelvie, kill_thebuddha & televoltmusic
events.description:
Join us on Sunday, October 5th for a sweet lil show! 🐌🐌🐌

Doors at at 7/music at 8/$10 suggested 

We’re welcoming jacobcmckelvie on tour from Massachusetts, alongside Greensboro’s own kill_thebuddha & televoltmusic 

jacobcmckelvie has traveled the DIY touring circuit in the US and UK for over a decade, carving out a niche for himself as a songwriter and performer. Known for his off-kilter and whimsical “stream of self-consciousness” lyricism and charming stage demeanor, he keeps audiences engaged both as a solo performer or with his newly minted backing band, “the New Old Band”. 

He released his latest LP “A New Kind of Hat” in 2024 and has been touring in support of that since.

Jake’s music has been featured on Americana UK, WFMU, Daytrotter, No Depression and has opened for acts like AJJ, Jeffrey Lewis, Clem Snide, Jeff Rosenstock and Steve Forbert.

“If you like a literate singer-songwriter with a witty turn of phrase then Jake is your man.” - Americana UK

“It just slyly worms its way into your brain.” - No Depression

See ya at the show! 🧃

events.title: 🎨 DAGGERTEETH
events.description:
DAGGERTEETH TOMORROW TOMORROW TOMORROW.
.
ARE Y'ALL PUMPED CUZ WE CAN'T WAIT TO OPEN OUR DOORS TO YOU TOMORROW!
.
Teeth don't lie. They bite, they tear, they mark what they touch.

Traditional printmaking techniques meet zine culture's raw urgency. Punk aesthetics slash through the white cube aesthetic and screams to be heard.

Every piece here carries evidence of hands—ink rolled, paper pressed, screens burned, images carved. The tactility of making things physical in a digital age. These works don't apologize for their rough edges. They weaponize them.

DAGGERTEETH celebrates artists who create outside institutional approval, distribution systems built on photocopiers and kitchen tables, aesthetics born from necessity and defiance. The underground made visible.

Sharp work for sharp teeth.

**February 21, 2026 | 5PM-8PM**

Irving Park Art & Frame

2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC

events.title: 🎨 H2HMRKT : HOLIDAY 2025
events.location: ONE REP, 207 S Westgate Dr STE A
events.description:
FLASHBACK FRIDAY ✨ Did you know that the first ever GSOZF was actually an extension of handtohandmarket and started in 2016?  The H2HMRKT is how I started producing events and it began in 2011.  It was the largest indie craft fair in Greensboro up until 2020 when the pandemic happened. Slowly but surely I have been coming back to event production and last year was the start of this when GSOZF came back. Now, I’m ready to bring back the #handtohandmarket because of onerepweightlifting expanding. 

H2HMRKT : HOLIDAY 2025
12/06-12/07 Sat. & Sun. 10:00-2:00PM
At ONE REP  207 S Westgate Dr STE A

If you’re looking for another opportunity to sling zines and your handmade goods, APPLY 🙌🩷.
Last day to do so is tonight at midnight. 
Link to register in profile!

events.title: 🎵 The BeanTones
events.description:
Tonight, Friday the 29th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Riding a wave of viral success, The BeanTones are redefining vocal jazz for a new generation. Drawing comparisons to legendary harmony groups like The Hi-Lo’s and The Four Freshmen, this dynamic ensemble has captivated global audiences with their inventive arrangements, flawless blend, and infectious energy.

events.title: 🎵 Tommy Prine with Brad Goodall
events.description:
Tomorrow, Saturday the 30th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Tommy Prine is a Nashville-bred songwriter carving out his own identity with unflinching honesty, dynamic guitar work, and a voice that bridges Americana, indie folk, and Southern storytelling. Following the breakout of his acclaimed debut album This Far South and the momentum around his new EP Love Circle (out now), Prine has emerged as one of the most compelling young artists in the scene — known for emotionally charged live shows, sharp songwriting, and a rapidly growing fanbase across the U.S. and Europe.

West Virginia songwriter and piano bard Brad Goodall established himself as a vivid storyteller with his 2022 debut Made In America, which boasted uproariously satirical character studies of sad, desperate men in the vein of Randy Newman and Warren Zevon. Where that album unfolded like a collection of biting short stories, his latest Hometown plays out like an autobiography..

events.title: 🎵 Kevn Kinney Live Performance
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 28th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Kevn Kinney is most widely known as founder and frontman of Atlanta-based rock band DrivinN-Cryin. A highly-acclaimed singer, songwriter, musician, performer, visual artist and poet, he 
has recently been inducted into both the Wisconsin Music Hall of Fame and Georgia Music Hall 
of Fame for his impactful legacy of art.
Kinney has released multiple critically acclaimed solo and band recordings during his career 
having also collaborated with Warren Haynes, REM, The Who and John Popper, among others. 
Peter Buck of REM has produced and performed on several of Kevn’s albums and continues to 
be involved in his career.
Kevn Kinney has continuously toured full-time with his band Drivin n Cryin while simultaneously 
carving out a niche for himself as a solo artist. From the festival stage to the smallest house 
concert, fans are always excited at the opportunity to see him up close and personal in a 
stripped-down acoustic setting. 
Recent touring include multiple dates with the Indigo Girls, Blackberry Smoke, The Black 
Crowes and others.
His latest solo studio album Think About It was released in 2022. In Fall 2023 the first release of 
the Let’s Go Dancin’: A Compilation Celebrating The Songs Of Kevn Kinney four-album vinyl 
series came out with over 100 digital recorded titles slated to go through end of 2024. The 
project is a massive all-star tribute to songwriter, poet, performer Kevn Kinney. Tracks from 
Jason Isbell, Violent Femmes, Shovels & Rope, Mike Farris, Great Peacock and dozens more 
will be released.

events.title: 🎵 Jill Andrews Performance
events.description:
This Saturday the 30th!
Doors at 2:30pm
Show at 3pm

Bold, infectious, introspective music that has served as the backdrop to some of America’s most beloved television series including Grey’s Anatomy, This Is Us, Nashville, and Wynnona Earp, to which she composed the theme.

“Andrews’ songs are beautifully crafted and the production molds even the more pensive selections into minor masterpieces” — American Songwriter

“Jill Andrews is one of the strongest young female songwriters around.” — Paste Magazine

events.title: 🎵 A Tribute to Dylan & Cash
events.description:
Today, Sunday the 24th!
Doors at 2:30pm
Show at 3pm

A Tribute to Dylan & Cash celebrates the legacy of two American giants. Created by Award-winning songwriter Rod Abernethy, recipient of 2019 Grand Prize winner of American Songwriter’s Bob Dylan Song Contest and David Burney, frontman of the acclaimed touring band Johnny Folsom 4. Together, they bring the lyrical genius and unmistakable voices of Dylan & Cash to life with unforge@able performances, rich storytelling and visuals. It’s a moving journey through the music, history, and the enduring friendship of Bob Dylan and Johnny Cash. Rod Abernethy is an authenFc southern folk troubadour, master acousFc guitarist, and award winning songwriter and composer for film, TV and video games. Rod’s awards include the Grand Prize winner of the 2021 InternaAonal AcousAc Music Awards. His album Normal Isn’t Normal Anymore received rave reviews from No Depression, American Highways and The Wall Street Journal and was in the Top 20 CD of 2021 at No. 11 on the Folk InternaAonal Folk Charts. David Burney’s lilFng southern baritone delivers Cash’s classic songs so well you may well wonder from Fme to Fme if Johnny himself is in the building. David has been the lead singer for Johnny Folsom 4 for over a decade and while nobody really sounds like Johnny Cash, nobody sounds more like Johnny Cash than Johnny Folsom 4. No Depression magazine says “For those of us unable to have seen Cash perform during his life, this is more than a treat. It’s a Ame machine. When it comes to honoring Johnny Cash, JF4 walks the line.”

events.title: 🎵 Jameson Tank, Lennon KC, Melodramatic
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 21st!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

With their electrifying arena rock sound and explosive stage presence, Jameson Tank has cemented itself as one of the most exciting rock bands to emerge from Athens, GA, this decade.

Lennon KC is a DIY alternative rock artist from Durham, North Carolina, currently playing as a duo. His sound flows between soft introspective moments and punk-inspired intensity, drawing comparisons to artists like Dominic Fike, The White Stripes, and Twenty One Pilots. His stage presence amplifies these contrasts, creating an experience that is both energized and intimate. Releasing his debut album in 2025, Lennon KC’s ‘Manic Pixie Dream Boy’ navigates selfhood with a soundscape featuring 90’s grunge, indie rock, hip-hop, and bedroom pop.

Melodramatic is the stage name and project of North Carolina songwriter Tyler Spencer, whose music blurs the lines between indie rock, folk, and experimental pop — inspired by artists like Slowdive, Japanese Breakfast, and Neil Young. What began as a bedroom recording outlet has grown into a full band rooted in the Southeast DIY scene, shaped by years of playing shows across NC and the surrounding region.

events.title: 🎵 Third Tuesday Night at the Flat Iron with Time Sawyer, Kray Van Kirk, Abigail Dowd
events.description:
Tonight, Tuesday the 19th!
Doors at 6:30pm
Show at 7:30pm

Sam Tayloe Through his full band vehicle, Time Sawyer, Sam Tayloe is interested in “real people and real songs” and that’s just what the listener finds in his music – a sense of realness. Blending a grassroots feel with heart-felt lyrics, Tayloe pulls the listener in with introspective ballads that land in that rootsy sweet spot where folk, alt-country, and rock gather for a good time.
 
Kray Van Kirk  is a gifted songwriter whose allegorical weave of contemporary and mythological themes is both remarkable and tremendously relevant. He backs that up with deft guitar work and a voice that embodies power and genuine tenderness. A steller and meticulous musician, equally rooted in the mind and the heart.” 
 
Abigail Dowd 

Our fearless host who still enjoys music most when it’s embedded in real life, sitting around a fire or every third Tuesday night at the Flat Iron. Each month Abigail invites musician friends to fall into space with her and the audience for what she describes as an evening in her adopted living room. They collaborate, share new songs, play old favorites, make things up, make you cry, make you laugh and most of all make you glad you came

events.title: 🎵 Eric Johanson
events.description:
Tomorrow, Wednesday the 20th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Eric Johanson stands at the forefront of modern blues with a sound that fuses deep groove, raw guitar fire, and a forward-leaning rock edge. His Ruf Records debut, The Deep and the Dirty, surged to #1 on the Billboard Blues Charts, marking a breakout moment that cemented Johanson as one of the genre’s most vital new voices. Leading a high-octane power trio, he performed in over 100 cities across 10 countries in 2024 alone. In 2025, Johanson followed with another Top 10 Billboard release (Live In Mississippi), extensive U.S. touring, and sold-out headline shows across Europe, while his reputation for electrifying live performances and word-of-mouth buzz continues to fuel rapid audience growth. From Grammy-nominated collaborations with Big Chief Monk Boudreaux, to direct support for artists like The Record Company, Samantha Fish, Tab Benoit, Anders Osborne, and North Mississippi Allstars, Johanson’s work has earned growing recognition from peers and critics alike. With a new studio album slated for 2026, Johanson continues to push modern blues into bold, unfiltered territory

events.title: 🎵 Rough Romance Tour: Flagship Romance and The Rough & Tumble
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 17th!
Doors at 6:30pm
Show at 7:30pm

Two internationally touring folk/Americana duos, The Rough& Tumble and Flagship Romance combine for a night of high energy, four part harmonies, infectious hooks and a roomful of laughter. It took over 10 years and 500,000 miles of touring the indie/folk circuit and seeing each other’s posters on venue walls before the two bands met. But it only took one meeting before they’d started a text thread and concocted the idea of the Rough Romance tour. It’s a night of open-armed, deep-in-the-belly-songs that are as intimate as they are universal and as delightful as your first cup of coffee. It’s a jubilant show that has become thehighlight of the year for many people to watch these four accomplished musicians and friends share the stage. 

Flagship Romance is an unforgettable alternative folk duo known for their breathtaking vocal harmonies, inspired songwriting, dynamic live show, and a remarkable on and off-stage chemistry that will leave you with the feeling that you made two new best friends. Married singers-songwriters Shawn Fisher and Jordyn Jackson effortlessly fuse technical precision in their impeccably tight vocal blend with an invigorating sense of emotional abandon in their energetic live persona. Performing each show as if it could be their last, this duo will have you questioning how their larger-than-life sound can come from just two voices and an acoustic guitar.

The Rough & Tumble are a hardscrabble folk/Americana duo, made up of Mallory Graham and Scott Tyler. With accordions and guitars, close-harmonies, ukuleles and oddball percussion, their commanding stage presence and engaging performances have made them a not-to-miss band when they tour through town because you won’t see the same show twice. They have been embraced by fans for their stark honesty, unique storytelling and well-crafted melodies especially after 2024’s release, Hymns For My Atheist Sister and Her Friends To Sing Along To, a collection of deeply personal, unifying and uplifting, humanist gospel songs.

events.title: 🤝 Greensboro Public Library Anime Con
events.description:
Greensboro Public Library Anime Con is delighted to announce our next featured panelist!

Dr. Clair Nguyen serves as Lecturer in Music Theory at UNC Greensboro. Her primary research focuses on the intersections of music theory and film & media studies that includes films, music videos, video games, theme park music, Japanese anime, and Hatsune Miku rhythm games. Her secondary research area centers on Vietnamese popular and traditional music, including the vọng cổ tradition.  She currently serves as advisor for the UNCG Animation Club as well as co-chair for the Society of Music Theory’s Film and Multimedia interest group.

She will be presenting her panel "The Intense Audiovisuals of Hatsune Miku," an exploration of how synchronized sounds and onscreen action create thoughtful and powerful storytelling in Vocaloid and Hatsune Miku: Colorful Stage! music videos.

Come meet Clair and join in on the fun on Saturday, August 8 from 10 am to 5 pm!

events.title: 🤝 Everything Everywhere All at Once Screening
events.description:
Our AAPI Heritage Month Cinema series concludes with a screening of "Everything Everywhere All at Once", the 2022 Best Picture winner directed by Daniel Kwan and Daniel Scheinert starring Michelle Yeoh, Ke Huy Kwan, and Stephanie Tsu.

A middle-aged Chinese immigrant is swept up into an insane adventure in which she alone can save existence by exploring other universes and connecting with the lives she could have led.

The film is rated R and this program is for adults.

📍 Hemphill Branch
📆 Sunday, May 31
⏰ 3 pm

events.title: 🤝 Greensboro Public Library Closed for Memorial Day
events.description:
All locations of the Greensboro Public Library will be closed on Monday, May 25 for Memorial Day.
----
Todas las ubicaciones de la Biblioteca Pública de Greensboro estarán cerradas el lunes, 25 de mayo por el Día de los Caídos.

events.title: 🤝 Dreams - Film Screening
events.description:
Our AAPI Heritage Month Cinema series continues with a screening of "Dreams", an anthology film directed by acclaimed Japanese filmmaker Akira Kurosawa.

Eight visually rich vignettes drawn from Kurosawa’s own dreams—fox weddings and vanished orchards, a soldier’s ghosts, a walk through Van Gogh’s canvases, nuclear nightmares, and a water-mill utopia—meditate on childhood, art, mortality, and humanity’s uneasy bond with nature.

The film is rated PG.

📍 Hemphill Branch
📆 Saturday, May 30
⏰ 3 pm

events.title: 🤝 Bike Storytime with Ms. Cindy
events.description:
May is National Bike Month, a chance to spotlight the many benefits of bicycling and an opportunity to encourage people to try biking, whether for transportation or recreation. 🚲

Join Ms. Cindy for stories about all kinds of bikes, from tricycles to bicycles! Enjoy books, rhymes, and songs in this interactive program for the whole family based on the five skills of Every Child Ready to Read: reading, writing, talking, singing, and playing.

📍 Glenwood Branch
📆 Saturday, May 30
⏰ 10:30 - 11:15 am

All ages, including adults, are welcome! Book selections will be provided, but you can also bring your own favorite books. Registration for a time slot is required - call 336-373-2015.

📍 Glenn McNairy Branch
📆 Saturday, May 30
⏰ 3 - 5 pm

events.title: 🤝 The Notebook Musical
events.location: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
events.description:
⭐ SPECIAL GIVEAWAY ⭐

"The Notebook", the best-selling novel by North Carolina resident Nicholas Sparks, has been adapted into a musical and will be performed at the Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts in June. They have donated TWO tickets as well as a goodie bag (including tissues for those tearjerker moments 💔) to the 7:30 pm showing on Tuesday, June 2 that we are giving away!

Here's how to enter:
1️⃣ Visit the registration desk at Central Library (219 N. Church St., Greensboro).
2️⃣ Fill out a ticket with your name and contact information.
3️⃣ OPTIONAL: Browse our collection, make a library card (if you don't already have one), and check out some items like a Nicholas Sparks book or the movie adaptation on DVD!

**This giveaway will end on May 26 when Central Library closes at 9 pm.

* Please note: winner will be chosen at random. Winner must respond within 48 hours or a runner-up will be chosen. This page (gsolibrary) is our only official Instagram account. Winner will be contacted via email (amy.bacon@greensboro-nc.gov) or phone (336-373-2957). Please be careful of fake spam accounts. This promotion is in no way sponsored, endorsed or administered by, or associated with Facebook, Instagram, Meta, or X/Twitter.

events.title: 🤝 Author Spotlight: Leigh Ann Henion - Night Magic
events.description:
✨ Author Spotlight! ✨

New York Times best-selling author Leigh Ann Henion, will share insights from her book, "Night Magic: Adventures Among Glowworms, Moon Gardens, and Other Marvels of the Dark". Known for her immersive explorations of the natural world, Henion invites readers to embrace the beauty and mystery of nature after dark. 🌙

Henion and environmental educator C.T. Finney will discuss the power of darkness in the lives of so many animals, and the deep connections between storytelling and environmental awareness. After the discussion, participants can take part in a guided outdoor activity.

ℹ Email melanie.buckingham@greensboro-nc.gov or call 336-373-2923 to register.

events.title: 🤝 End-of-Year Raffle
events.description:
🎁 End-of-Year Raffle Time!
We wanted to thank you for showing up and reading with us.
✨ At our next meeting — Sunday, Dec 7 at 12pm at scuppernongbooks — we’ll be hosting a raffle with three prize bundles.
Each prize includes:

📚 a Blind Date With a Book
🌿 + bookish goodies from local small businesses we love (boroughcoffeegso chandlersdtgreensboro thetinygreenhouse scuppernongbooks )

Here’s how to enter:
✨ If you’ve attended 6 or more meetings this year, just give us your name at our Sunday meet up.

✨ Can’t make it this weekend? Send us a DM to let us know you’ve hit 6 meetings, and we’ll add you to the raffle.

If you’re just now joining us, we’d still like to see you on Sunday! 

✨ Thanks for reading with us.
.
.
.

events.title: 🛍️ Next Event
events.description:
Merry Christmas & Happy Holidays 🎄 As we approach the end of the year , we are thankful for all of the love and support we’ve gotten thus far. It’s never easy starting something new, you’ve gotta just put your best foot forward and go. But with God, the right people around you and a community-centric town like Greensboro, there’s no way to lose ❤️ so I appreciate everyone who has shared a post, told a friend, or dragged a family member to one of our events. We’ve done a lot in 9 months thanks to y’all, looking forward to what the next year brings! 

And since it’s the giving season we figured we’d give you the date of our next event 😄 February 1st! More details and vendor information coming soon . Stay Tuned, Stay Warm, Stay Safe! 🫡

📸 | shottbyverde

 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists Special Meeting
events.description:
Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists would like to invite you to join us this Monday, February 10 at 7pm at 1333 Grove St for a special meeting, featuring a talk by organizer Kristen Godfrey on their article, "Borders are prisons and prisons are borders" with a discussion to follow.

*

"The way working-class people experience borders all over the world is tied to the theft of land and surplus value. Settler-colonialism’s life source is criminality and illegality, which is why we must be anti-capitalist abolitionists, developing a critical analysis of how imperialists’ tactics work to divide us."

*

Tell us how this new context impacts you. Let's think about how we can resist together.
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🎵 Latour & Jimmy D pres. “Spotlight” Launch Party
events.description:
Latour & Jimmy D pres. “Spotlight” Launch Party tonight at elsewhere with an extended 3.5hr b2b. Support from Rav & Cobella

Golden Hour 9-10

events.title: 🎵 WAXX OFF at Elsewhere
events.description:
Welcoming the debut to elsewhere of Australia’s homegrown UKG star waxxoffmusic this July. Tickets on sale in bio now

WAXX OFF is a frontline dj and producer from adelaide, south australia, shaping australia’s current wave of underground uk garage and speed garage. renowned for high-impact, high energy performances, his sets command the dance-floor through purposeful, momentum-driven programming. his productions have gained global traction through releases on influential labels including ec2a, sweat it out and no instruction, earning support from key figures such as interplanetary criminal, malugi, marlon hoffstadt and dr dubplate just to name just a few.following a year of relentless touring throughout australia, new zealand and asia in 2025; including standout performances at a3 festival, beyond the valley and wildlands, waxx off has firmly established himself as one of the country’s most exciting producers and selectors.beyond his solo output, his work with ukg outfit inside kru has delivered beatport top 100 charting records and multiple national sellouts, further solidifying his position within the scene entering 2026 with a wave of new music and clear momentum, waxx off is positioned for his biggest year to date

events.title: 🎵 Record Launch Party: Spotlight by Latour & Jimmy D
events.description:
This Saturday we host the launch party of the new joint record from elsewhere’s two music directors Latour + Jimmy D, who’ve teamed up to release “Spotlight” on the iconic Central Station Records. 

Latour b2b Jimmy D for a 3.5hr set this Saturday
with support from Rav & Cobella. 

About the record:
Latour and Jimmy D are two music directors on the Gold Coast at elsewhere who’ve come together to write Spotlight. (their second collab)

Jimmy’s history dates back to being a vinyl DJ on the Sydney circuit in the 90’s, we wanted to bring back the flavour of that era with new updated NRG, that holds weight on today’s systems and events. 

The record being a standout memorable tune of the night with its stellar vocal which sees the majority of the record stripped away leaving a sassy ear catching moment in the rave. Then slowly creeping back up to an all our NRG finale. Think donk basslines, 90s rave synths & fast grooving raw drums.

A record to make SURE the dancefloor is indeed dancing. And if you aren’t then, “getttt the f offfffff “

events.title: 🎵 Hi_Mija Afterparty at Untold
events.description: tonight we link with our friends over at untold to host the afterparty headlined by hi_mija , doors open to all from 9pm for all things techno, bounce, hard groove & more

events.title: 🎵 blake.08
events.description:
This Saturday we welcome back anjunadeep artist blake.08_  no online tickets, door only from 9pm

blake.08 stands out as a rare breed of electronic music producer – one who balances underground credibility with broad appeal, and who has earned respect from dance music’s veterans while staying firmly grounded in his own vision. from humble beginnings in a sydney bedroom studio to gracing international stages, blake gilray’s journey is a testament to patience, authenticity, and passion for the art. with a global cult following behind him, support from industry heavyweights, and a debut album on the way, 2025 is poised to be the year blake.08 cements his place among electronic music’s exciting new leaders. whether he’s dropping a late-night club heater or a blissful downtempo anthem, blake.08 does it on his own terms – and the world is listening

events.title: 🎵 Country & Rave
events.description:
🔥 LAST WEEKEND OF MAY AT THE GRAND GSO 🔥
4 nights. 4 different vibes. One destination.
117 S Elm St • Downtown Greensboro

🤠 THURSDAY – COUNTRY & RAVE
Neon country meets EDM energy at Little Saloon.
Live music, dance floor vibes, $5 house drinks & $6 Casamigos all night.

💦 FRIDAY – INTERNATIONAL WET TSHIRT CONTEST
Hosted by DJ Kevin P.
Ladies free with online ticket.
$1,000 CASH PRIZE to the top contestant.
Doors open at 10PM + drink specials all night.

🔥 SATURDAY – NUEVA VS VIEJA ESCUELA
The battle of generations returns.
Old school hits vs new school anthems all night long.
Ladies free before 11PM.

🍻 SUNDAY – SUNDAY FUN DAY
Trivia night, live music, games, margaritas & beer buckets at Little Saloon.
The perfect Sunday downtown vibe with friends.

🎟️ RSVP & Tickets online
📍 The Grand GSO – Greensboro, NC
Who’s outside with us this weekend?

events.title: 🤝 Downtown Signal Box Student Art Project Celebration
events.description:
These aren’t just signal boxes. They’re canvases!

The Downtown Signal Box Student Art Project brought 47 pieces of original artwork from local Greensboro students in kindergarten through 12th grade to the streets of downtown, and we want the community to come celebrate!

Join us at Center City Park on June 2nd from 5 to 7 PM for prizes, a map to see all the art, and more! 

Have you noticed the art around downtown?

events.title: 🤝 Allegacy Financial Fun Fourth Freedom Run
events.description:

The Allegacy Financial Fun Fourth Freedom Run registration is now OPEN. Pick your distance and hit the pavement:

1 Mile | 5K | 10K

Starting at Center City Park, with the Allegacy Financial Freedom Fest waiting for you at the finish line right downtown.

Register now at the link in bio and send this reel to your running buddies!

events.title: 🤝 Downtown Greenway Grand Opening Ribbon Cutting Celebration

events.description:

Cairn’s Course is located between Guilford Ave and West Friendly Ave!

events.title: 🤝 CultureWorks
events.location: Greensboro Cultural Center and LeBauer Park
events.description:
Meet Kiarah of JAVONNE by Kiarah Javonne, one of 9 local designers showcasing at CultureWorks this May 23rd.

Produced by Creative Greensboro, CultureWorks is a free, day-long festival shining a spotlight on the city’s artists, designers, and creatives. Taking place at the Greensboro Cultural Center and LeBauer Park, 11am–8pm.

events.title: 🌈 Heidi N Closet at Chemistry
events.description:
⚠️ ATTENTION! ⚠️

In the interest of public safety and overcrowding concerns, we’ve had to make some difficult decisions regarding Heidi N Closet’s return to the Chemistry stage.

We truly appreciate all the excitement surrounding this event, and we know Heidi appreciates the incredible support from her hometown crowd as well! However, to ensure everyone’s safety and to remain within venue occupancy limits, the following changes will be in effect:

💲 Cover Charges (21+ Only)
• FREE before 9 PM
• $10 from 9:00 PM – 11:59 PM
• $20 from Midnight until Close

🚫 This event is strictly 21+
Guests ages 18–20 will not be permitted entry.

Thank you for understanding and helping us keep this event safe and enjoyable for everyone. We can’t wait to welcome our girl home and party with you!
 Greensboro's LGBTQ+ nightclub

events.title: 🌈 Power of Pride Celebration
events.description:
Pride month is just around the corner and we're gearing up for our annual "Power of Pride" campaign! This is a month-long fundraiser which supports our work providing resources, programming, and events for a thriving LGBTQ community. 
🌈We're CELEBRATING Pride month and this campaign with a multi-generational event planned for June 20th at The Pyrle theater: LINK in BIO
💲Donate at least $50 to The Power of Pride campaign and get FREE ADMISSION to the celebration on the 20th!
👕New T-shirt Teaser! CHECK THE COMMENTS for a look at the new Power of Pride shirts. These will be for sale at the Center and at our community appearances and events.
🎉Stay tuned for the kick-off of the fundraising campaign on June 1st.
 Advancing equality and inclusion through philanthropy, programming, and advocacy that uplifts the LGBTQ community.

events.title: 🌈 GAYme night
events.description:
Hey, you! Yes, you - GAYme night is happening at the GGF Center this Saturday 6-10pm, and it is free and open to adults 18 and older who enjoy a good card or board game in a safe and welcoming space with great company.

This week's featured games are Arkham Horror and Nemesis, and we'll have traditional fan favorites as well! Bring a friend or come solo - we'll have a seat for you!
 Advancing equality and inclusion through philanthropy, programming, and advocacy that uplifts the LGBTQ community.

events.title: 🌈 Pride Celebration at Pyrle Theater
events.description:
Are you ready to celebrate Pride?!? Pride in our gender identity, gender expression, and sexual orientation happens year-round, and it's also great to have opportunities to celebrate together!

Join us at the Pyrle Theater in downtown Greensboro for a day of celebration on Saturday, June 20. The fun kicks off in the afternoon with family-friendly songs led by Mr. Sam of Scuppernong's Saturday Get-Downs, followed by a pizza dance party for youth and teens. To close out the night, the 21+ crowd will be treated to performances by drag superstars Thigh Fieri, Franny Starlight, and Brenda the Drag Queen, with DJ SmoothE providing tunes throughout the evening.

The Pyrle will have food and drinks available to keep the fun going. Tickets will be available soon through the Pyrle, so stay tuned!
 Advancing equality and inclusion through philanthropy, programming, and advocacy that uplifts the LGBTQ community.

events.title: 🌈 First Tuesdays with Friends
events.description:
Join us for the KICKOFF of "First Tuesdays with Friends" TONIGHT, Tuesday April 7.  This is a new monthly social presented by GGF and Lewis & Elm that will be held on the first Tuesday of each month. Come enjoy some wine, cocktails, tasty non-alcoholic options, and delicious food in downtown Greensboro's Southend.
Lewis & Elm will be donating 15% of cocktail and mocktail sales to promote the vision of GGF to create a thriving LGBTQ community in the Triad.
This new monthly event will be in addition-to our Takeovers, where we will hop around the Triad.
The evening at Lewis & Elm is drop-in style between 5:30-7:30. Come when you can, leave when you must! See you tonight!

Tickets at LINK in BIO
PROMOTIONS: 
-Post on social media about your ticket purchase before 3 pm, tag guilfordgreenfoundation, show us the post at the merch table and get a FREE GAME DAUBER
-The first 150 in the doors tonight get a FREE PROGRESS PRIDE FLAG.
-All Bingo players will be eligible to win CASH prizes provided by Truliant Federal Credit Union, Merch from Charlie's Soap, and Tickets to Blue Man Group at The Tanger Center

events.title: 🍽️ National Homeless Persons' Memorial Walk
events.description:
Wednesday, December 17th, at 5pm people will gather at Greensboro Urban Ministry for the National Homeless Persons’ Memorial Walk. The event is to honor our neighbors who have died homeless. 

The walk will be down Gate City Blvd and end at Grace Community Church where the names of those who we have lost this year will be read aloud, afterwards dinner will be served. 

Greensboro Urban Ministry - 305 w gate city blvd
Grace Community Church - 643 w gate city blvd
 Mutual aid & community fridge in Greensboro.

events.description:

events.title: 🤝 The Foursome Book Discussion with Christina Baker Kline
events.location: The Virginia Somerville Sutton Theatre at Well-Spring
events.description:
What happens when one of history’s most unusual true stories becomes a novel?

Find out when Christina Baker Kline joins Greensboro Bound to discuss “The Foursome,” her new book inspired by the lives of Eng and Chang Bunker and their connection to North Carolina history. 

Thursday, June 4
2:00 PM
The Virginia Somerville Sutton Theatre at Well-Spring

events.title: 🤝 Social Saturday Trolley Pub Tour
events.description:
June 6th Social Saturday we are doing a curated Trolley Pub tour to take you to some of the great art stops in Downtown High Point! 

The tour starts as 2pm at Studio 136 and goes to Rushton Gallery, TAG (The Art Gallery), and J.H. Adams Inn Gallery 

There will be delicious drinks to purchase along the way 

To register your slot, email Tyler@dthp.org 

This experience is first-come, first-serve. There are only 11 slots, so be sure to register today! 

#DowntownHP #HighPointNC #SocialSaturday #TriadNC #ArtWalk

events.title: 🤝 Art & Ale: Social Saturday
events.description:

events.title: 🤝 Social Saturday: Art & Ale
events.description:

We'll be running 2 trolley routes so you don't miss a thing 🚎

We're making it easy to support local this Social Saturday! 

#DowntownHP #highpointNC #SocialSaturday #SupportLocal #LocalArtists

events.title: 🤝 Social Saturday Scavenger Hunt
events.description:
Social Saturday is TODAY! 

Take a ride on the free trolley to each of our participating locations 🚎

Keep an eye for the FIVE Downtown Dollars Gift Cards that have been hidden at participating locals serving flights

We'll see you out in Downtown today and Happy Hunting! 

#DowntownHP #highpointNC #SocialSaturday #ScavengerHunt

events.title: 🤝 Social Saturday: Bubbles and Bourbon
events.description:
This Social Saturday we are popping bottles 🍾 and keeping it barrel aged 🥃 with our Bubbles and Bourbon theme! 

Keep your eye out for FIVE Downtown HP Dollars Gift Cards! We're hiding four $25 and one $100 gift cards around town. Here's your hint: They'll be at participating locations that are serving flights

So check out our website to see which locations you'll be hitting up and happy hunting! 

#DowntownHP #highpointNC #SocialSaturday #LocalEvents

events.description:
DPAC Fan Friday 🌟 HAMILTON Edition 🪶
This week, we asked our guests to finish the lyric 🎵 and they didn't throw away their shot 👏
Don't miss America's #1 Musical, HAMILTON, as part of WRAL's Greatest Hits of Broadway.
Tickets start at $52 (+ Tax) at DPACnc.com. Good seats remain for the June 9 - 14 evening performances.
DPAC presented by First Horizon

events.title: 🎭 79th Annual Tony Awards
events.description:
🏆The Road to the Tony Awards🏆
In just 10 days, Broadway's biggest night arrives - The 79th Annual Tony Awards ✨
All week long, we will be spotlighting this season's biggest nominees, beginning with The Lost Boys.  This show is the most Tony nominated musical of the year, featuring electrifying music, stunning visuals, and spectacular special effects. 
Tune in Sunday, June 7 on CBS, Paramount+ or DPAC's social channels as we celebrate the best of Broadway ✨

events.title: 🤝 Lambs & Clams Reunion at the Finale
events.description:
It's been incredible to be back in Charleston for the 20th anniversary of chswineandfood, and to share this time with echolocate (my oldest kiddo). 

Tonight's our main event: the Lambs & Clams reunion at the Finale! 

But first these two introverted extroverts need to find a quiet beach to recharge our batteries.

events.title: 🎨 Still. Moving: Zonal Harmonic 170,000 and Zonal Harmonic 220,000
events.description:
Interpreted by Rainbow Dance Company Director of Dance Kathryn Auman, this Saturday's free "Still. Moving: Zonal Harmonic 170,000 and Zonal Harmonic 220,000" performance will explore the unseen architectures that shape how we move, gather, and belong.

Inspired by Tomás Saraceno’s vision of airborne networks and interspecies connection, Rainbow Dance/ArtsTogether will perform a piece that considers the body not as an isolated figure but as part of a larger, vibrating system—held in tension, resonance, and relation.

A brief talkback with Auman and the dancers follows the performance.

Join us in West Building at either 11 am or 1 pm on May 30 to see art in motion - and watch the video to see clips of Rainbow Dance/Arts Together's previous Still. Moving interpretation of Yoan Capote's "Isla (Tierra Prometida)."

events.title: 🎨 NCMA Loves Anime Bazaar
events.description:
Our celebration of everything anime, NCMA Loves Anime, returns on Saturday, June 13, and this summer's event is going to be bigger than ever!

NCMA Loves Anime will feature anime vendors, music, and food, followed by a ticketed evening screening of Mamoru Hosoda's 2009 film "Summer Wars."

We will have both new vendors, such as PlantformerCreations, MsBobaCaps, and CaitlynKurilich, and returning favorites, like Boxuchan, ChunkieBunnieArt, and plenty more. The CrispyGyoza food truck will be on site from 11:30 am to 2 pm and from 5 to 8 pm.

Check out the video for a quick tour of NCMA Loves Anime's winter bazaar.

Note: Cosplayers are welcome, but please leave the weapons/props at home and keep in mind that this is an all-ages, family-friendly event!

-------
Note: This post has been updated to reflect that the film screening is a ticketed event.

events.title: 🎨 Teen Night 2026
events.description:
We are very excited to announce the details for Teen Night 2026 at the NCMA!

Happening Friday, June 12, from 5:30 pm-8:30 pm:

Themed "Around the Campfire," teens 13-18 are invited to enjoy free admission to the Knowing the West exhibition, dress up in the style of the American West, line dance, participate in games and art, and more!

This event is FREE. Please RSVP at the link in our bio!

events.title: 🎨 From Irving Berlin to Louis Armstrong
events.description:
Tickets are running low for Thursday evening's concert, "From Irving Berlin to Louis Armstrong," featuring renowned internationally touring jazz pianist JoeAlterman and his quartet, plus special guest ChristianTamburrProductions!

This concert celebrates the extraordinary creative exchange between Jewish composers and African American performers that helped define the sound of the 20th century. Join us at 7 pm on May 28 as these musicians explore iconic works by legendary figures such as Irving Berlin, George Gershwin, Duke Ellington, and Louis Armstrong.

Get your tickets at the link in our bio before they sell out!

events.title: 🎨 Still. Moving dance performance
events.description:
Our next "Still. Moving" dance performance will be on Saturday, May 30, at 11 am and 1 pm in West Building. Created in dialogue with Tomás Saraceno’s "Zonal Harmonic 170,000" and "Zonal Harmonic 220,000," this original work by Rainbow Dance Co./Arts Together explores the unseen architectures that shape how we move, gather, and belong.

Inspired by Saraceno’s vision of airborne networks and interspecies connection, the choreography considers the body not as an isolated figure but as part of a larger, vibrating system—held in tension, resonance, and relation. Like Saraceno’s sculptural environments, the dance piece asks: What holds us up, and what holds us together?

Both performances are free to attend, and no registration is required. You can read more about this performance at the link in our bio.

--------
Image 1: Tomás Saraceno, Zonal Harmonic 170,000, 2022, carbon fiber, polyester rope, monofilament, H. 34 5/8 × W. 56 5/16 × D. 46 7/16 in., Gift of Richard DeWid and Helen Murray with additional funds from the North Carolina State Art Society (Robert F. Phifer Bequest) 

Tomás Saraceno, Zonal Harmonic 220,000, 2022, carbon fiber, polyester rope, monofilament, H. 73 5/8 × W. 103 15/16 × D. 102 3/8 in., Gift of Richard DeWid and Helen Murray with additional funds from the North Carolina State Art Society (Robert F. Phifer Bequest) 

Image 2: Rainbow Dance Co.: Courtesy of the company

events.title: 🎨 Knowing the West Community Day - Free Exhibition Entry
events.description:
Looking for something to do today, but don't feel like being out in the rain? You're in luck! 

Today is our Knowing the West Community Day, which means you get free entry into our new exhibition, Knowing the West. Additionally, from 11 am-4 pm, we will be hosting the Western North Carolina Art and Craft Showcase, a free one-day market celebrating the traditions and contemporary practices of craft artists from WNC, alongside makers from across the state.

Visit us today to get a new perspective on the American West, then meet North Carolina artists, explore handmade work, and experience what makes the NCMA a statewide gathering place for creativity, connection, and cultural celebration!

Entry to both events is free, and no registration is required.

-----------
Pictured: Olive Rush, Maria and Julian, 1914, oil on academy board, 12 1/4 × 18 1/2 in., Tia Collection, Santa Fe, NM; Image: James Hart Photography

events.title: 🎨 György Kurtág at 100: Hungarian and Romanian Folk-Inspired Music
events.description:
Soundcheck ahead of tonight's concert, "György Kurtág at 100: Hungarian and Romanian Folk-Inspired Music"! 

Join us at 7 pm in the SECU Auditorium, where MallarmeMusic presents works by Hungarian composers György Kurtág and Béla Bartók for cimbalom, violin, mezzo, and piano.

Tickets are available at the link in our bio.

events.title: 🎨 Community Day: Knowing the West and Western NC Art and Craft Showcase
events.description:
THIS SATURDAY, MAY 23: From 11 am-4 pm, join us for a community day featuring free admission to Knowing the West and a Western NC Art and Craft Showcase!

This one-day market, in partnership with BlueRidgeHeritage, celebrates the traditions and contemporary practices of craft artists from Western North Carolina, alongside makers from across the state.

Featuring ceramics, textiles, woodwork, jewelry, prints, and more, the showcase highlights the depth and diversity of North Carolina’s creative community.

The event invites visitors to meet artists, explore handmade work, and experience the NCMA as a statewide gathering place for creativity, connection, and cultural celebration.

Some of the featured artists/makers who will be on hand (in order of the photos) include:
RosaFriedrichs1230
PrideAndArchive
WishingFlowerPress
JL.MerrillMetalworks
Toryn_Black_Design
RedRenJewelry

We will also have CornerBoysBBQ here to serve up their fantastic barbecue and soul food!

Our Community Day is free to attend, and no registration is required. We'll see you on Saturday!

----------
Images: Courtesy of the artists

events.title: 🎨 249-year-old Revolutionary War sketch on view
events.description:
Beginning today, thanks to a loan from the AmRevMuseum, the NC Museum of Art will be the temporary home for a 249-year-old sketch depicting a North Carolina revolutionary war brigade as they marched through Philadelphia. The drawing is one of only 12 known eye-witness accounts of George Washington's Army.

As part of North Carolina's celebration of America's 250th anniversary, this sketch, likely created by Pierre Eugene du Simitiere in 1777, will be on view at the NCMA through August 1. It will then head to the N.C. History Center at TryonPalace through early October, before making its final loan stop at the Visitor Center at HistoricHalifaxNC through November 6.

Visit us today to see this extremely rare historic work in person!

Click the link in our bio to read more about the fascinating story behind this piece of history.
----------
Image: Courtesy of nc.dncr

events.title: 🎨 Boris Lurie: Echoes of Memory
events.description:
Looking for a mid-week music, history, and culture break? Join us this Wednesday from 1 pm-3 pm for a free program in tribute to artist, activist, Holocaust survivor, and art movement founder Boris Lurie.

"Boris Lurie: Echoes of Memory" is a powerful synthesis of historical music, visual art, archival testimony, and live performance that situates Lurie’s work within the broader artistic and intellectual responses to the Holocaust.

Featuring violinist Ilana Zaks-Nederlander, this event honors Lurie’s legacy with virtuosic solo violin works by Jewish composers persecuted during the Holocaust.

Stephanie Stebich, executive director of the Boris Lurie Art Foundation, gives opening remarks, and a Q&A with Zaks-Nederlander and Stebich concludes the program.

This is the inaugural event in a new series, Off the Wall, in which the Initiative for Jewish Art and Culture delves into the world of lesser-known modern and contemporary American artists.

Get your free ticket at the link in our bio.

This event is a program of the Initiative for Jewish Art and Culture and a collaboration with the Boris Lurie Art Foundation.

-----------
Pictured: Boris Lurie, LIBERATION OF MAGDEBURG, 1946, Pastel and gouache on paper, 19 x 25 in., Courtesy of the Boris Lurie Art Foundation

events.title: 🎨 Language Immersion Evening
events.description:
Interested in learning a new language? Join us TONIGHT for an evening of cultural immersion! Along with Raleigh Sister Cities, language ambassadors will be on hand to start you on your language-learning journey with a beginner-friendly approach. Spanish and Arabic are tonight's featured languages!

Refreshments and snacks will be available for purchase. No registration is required.

events.title: 🎨 Live Music by Thomas Hinds (Notes on View)
events.description:
This Saturday, May 16, at 11 am and noon, visit West Building to enjoy live music by Thomas Hinds (hindsight_musings) as he makes his return to the NCMA!

A heartfelt folk-rock troubadour, Hinds will perform his soul-baring, autobiographical songs in our galleries as a response to the works in the People's Collection.

These performances are part of our Notes on View series and are free to attend.

events.description: Don’t miss this chance to see a great play by the talented playwright and filmmaker Mark Cornell. events.title: 🎨 Horse Lords
events.description:
JUST ANNOUNCED: SUNDAY 10/11 8:00 PM — Horse Lords (local support TBD) 

Horse Lords were founded in Baltimore, Maryland in 2010; they evolved from the experimental collective Teeth Mountain and began as a trio with drummer Sam Haberman, guitarist Owen Gardner, and bassist Max Eilbacher, soon adding alto saxophonist Andrew Bernstein to the core ensemble. Though Horse Lords grew out of the fertile Baltimore noise and leftfield rock scene, a storied environment for artists and weirdos that has nurtured many an influential outsider band (Lungfish, Matmos), their approach is more omnivorous than the stippled rhythms of instrumental electric rock would indicate. 

Across sixteen years of frequent touring, Horse Lords have released ten studio, live, and collaboration albums in addition to a split EP with fellow Baltimoreans Lower Dens, a handful of singles and compilation tracks. For RVNG’s FRKWYS series, the band collaborated with Berlin-based composer Arnold Dreyblatt, a onetime student of La Monte Young and Pauline Oliveros whose just intonation chamber music was a crucial part of the post-Fluxus Downtown New York City environment. 

With the relocation of three of the four members to Germany beginning in 2021, the potential for more further creative exploration, and performance opportunities including the Moers Festival (with an 11-piece lineup, interpreting Julius Eastman’s music as well as their own), the interdisciplinary phases of the band’s music was poised for fascinating new directions. The band observes “We are always trying to balance working within the limitations of the quartet and pushing beyond them.

For their second studio album on RVNG Intl., Demand To Be Taken To Heaven Alive!, Horse Lords have followed this notion and augmented the instrumentation, expanding the format slightly to include bass clarinetist Madison Greenstone (TAK Ensemble), trombonist Weston Olencki (Nate Wooley, RAGE Thormbones) and vocalists Nina Guo (Departure Duo) and Evelyn Saylor (Holly Herndon, Caterina Barbieri) across a program of twelve original compositions. 

Read more/buy tickets at https://artscenterlive.org/performances-events

events.title: 🎨 Ansel Adams: A Man, A Monument, A Memory
events.description:
📸✨ Learn from an Internationally-Acclaimed Photographer This Spring!

The ArtsCenter is thrilled to welcome New Jersey-based photographer and mixed media artist Nancy Ori for a special series of online classes and lectures this April–June!

With decades of experience—including studying under legendary photographer Ansel Adams and exhibiting work in major collections like the Museum of Modern Art—Nancy brings a rare blend of expertise, creativity, and inspiration to every session.

🌿 Upcoming Online Classes (All Levels | Ages 15+)

📅 April 19 | 11:00 AM – 12:30 PM
Ansel Adams: A Man, A Monument, A Memory
💲 $48 ($43.20 members)
Explore the life and legacy of Ansel Adams through Nancy’s personal stories and experiences studying and teaching alongside him.

🎨 May 10 | 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM
Hand Coloring Your Inkjets
💲 $72 ($64.80 members)
Learn how to transform your photos into one-of-a-kind mixed media pieces using watercolor, pastels, and colored pencils—no experience needed!

🌸 May 17 | 11:00 AM – 12:30 PM
Nature As Inspiration for Art
💲 $48 ($43.20 members)
Discover how artists draw from nature to create everything from realism to abstraction.

💡 May 24 | 11:00 AM – 12:30 PM
The Art of Composition and Light
💲 $48 ($43.20 members)
Master the fundamentals of composition and lighting to elevate your photography or painting.

🤖 June 7 | 11:00 AM – 12:30 PM
Beyond the Buttons: Using AI as a Creative Tool
💲 $48 ($43.20 members)
Unlock the power of AI for creativity, productivity, and artistic exploration.

🌍 All classes are held online via Zoom—join from anywhere!

Spots are limited—don’t miss this opportunity to learn from a globally recognized artist and educator.

📩 Questions? artschool@artscenterlive.org

📩 Questions? artschool@artscenterlive.org

events.title: 🎨 Friday Night Vision Boarding with an Eye on the Summer
events.description:
🎨✨ **This Weekend at The ArtsCenter — Get Creative!**

Looking for something fun, hands-on, and inspiring to do this weekend? The ArtsCenter has **3 unique classes** coming up—perfect for trying something new or bringing a friend!

🌙 **Friday Night Vision Boarding with an Eye on the Summer**
📅 Friday, April 10 | 6:30–9:30 PM
💲 $72
Kick off your weekend with creativity and intention. Guided by a board-certified art therapist, this fun and inspiring workshop helps you visualize your goals and dream up your ideal summer.

🔨 **Make Your Own Tin Can Collage**
📅 Saturday, April 11 | 1:00–5:00 PM
💲 $91
Curious about working with metal? Create your own 8" x 10" collage using repurposed tin—no experience needed! A perfect mix of creativity and hands-on making.

📸 **iPhone Photo Safari at the Carrboro Farmers’ Market**
📅 Saturday, April 11 | 9:00–11:30 AM
💲 $72
Capture the colors of spring with your iPhone! Join an award-winning instructor for a guided photo walk and learn how to turn everyday snapshots into intentional images.

🌟 All classes are beginner-friendly (ages 15+) and designed to spark creativity.

👉 Spots are limited—grab a friend and sign up today!

events.title: 🎨 ArtsCenter Open House
events.description: Mark your calendars ! Sunday April 19th 2-4pm . Carrboro is going to be the place to be ! Wander over to us here at the ArtsCenter and get a taste of all that we offer 😎 ! Fun for the whole family ! art, music, ceramics, good beer on draft, James Wallace on piano - what could be better ?!

events.title: 🎨 Black Sheep Improv show
events.description: We are going to be closed tomorrow morning 4/3 ! We have the Spring Fever ! Come to see us when we open up at 4pm and don’t forget about the Black Sheep Improv show at 7:30 !! It feels great to laugh 😂. The Ceramics studio will operate as per usual. Happy Spring everyone ! 🐣

events.title: 🎨 Polaroid Emulsion Lift Workshop
events.description:
You don't know satisfaction until you've caught a wiggly, floating Polaroid emulsion lift on a piece of watercolor paper...

Join the amazing lindsaymetivier of peel_gallery and try out this fun technique for transferring your photos onto paper and turning them into works of art! You start with just a photo on your phone and end with a beautiful keepsake that can become a greeting card, part of a scrapbook, or could just be framed and kept as-is.

The workshop is this Thursday, April 2, from 6-8:30pm. Register at artscenterlive.org or by calling the ArtsCenter. 📸

events.description:
Hey DTWS, we have a new FRESH juice bar! Come out to the Grand Opening of The Jugo Bar today at 6pm! thejugoman 

🍏 Owner William Fulton is a Winston-Salem local and makes all of the juices on-site with “no junk, just juice” 

🍊 The Jugo bar serves fresh cold-pressed juice, tea, mushroom coffee, smoothies and smoothie bowls 

📍411 W. 4th St. #141

events.title: 🤝 Downtown Winston-Salem Walking Treasure Hunt
events.description:
🔍🕵️‍♀️Explore Downtown Winston-Salem on a walking treasure hunt! 

💰💵 Simply download the Xplorer Treasure Hunts mobile app, complete the hunt, and be entered for a chance to win a $100 gift card to a downtown business! Use activity code “wsalem” to play for free! Link in bio for deets 

🗓️ Treasure Hunt will be live until April 30th!

💵 **Ten winners will be drawn for a $100 gift certificate

🎥: triangletreasurehunt 
#DTWS #FinditHere

events.title: 🤝 Weekday Lunch Specials
events.description:
📍 istanbulgyroandburger (216 W. 4th St.) 

Weekday lunch specials are Monday–Friday 11am - 4pm starting at just $9.95 
(yes… we love a good deal 👏)

Stack your plate with:
🥙 Beef + chicken gyro combo plates
🌯 Lavash wraps
🍔 Cheeseburger & chicken burger combos
🥗 Fresh, flavorful salad options

And don’t even think about skipping dessert…
🍰 Tiramisu, chocolate cake, fruit cake
✨ Pistachio dream & Dubai chocolate cake
🍯 Turkish baklava + cannolis
🍦 Rotating ice cream flavors

Basically… come hungry, leave happy 🤝

events.title: 🎵 Nether Hour at Gas Hill Drinking Room
events.description:
Happy to welcome netherhour  in from Austin, Texas to Gas Hill Drinking Room on June 24, 2026! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets are on sale NOW at TheRamkat.com!

“We don’t play country music, but we’re good ole boys from the country.”

Down in the heart of Austin, TX, where the music runs thicker than the heat, there’s a band that’s been making some serious noise. They call themselves Nether Hour, and they’ve been turning every dive bar and backroad juke joint into a full-blown musical revival. The band is a trio of unapologetic rebels, each one a powerhouse of sonic defiance. Bobby Flaco delivers gritty vocals and razor-sharp guitar riffs, while Bishop unleashes searing lead guitar and mandolin work, with Bercy laying down bass lines that slam right into your gut. Nether Hour is a relentless sonic melting pot, fusing the swampy grooves of Louisiana with Texas-sized doses of funk, blues, soul, and rock ‘n’ roll. #netherhour #gashilldrinkingroom #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: 🎵 alolender in support of Fruit Bats
events.description:
Excited  to welcome alolender to The Ramkat on July 16, 2026 in support of Fruit Bats! 

Get your tickets NOW at TheRamkat.com!

Songwriter Al Olender tries to grasp the brief, beautiful moments that make up our lives in slow-motion but finds that the tighter she holds on, the more these frames fall into a soupy blur. Change is our only constant, but what if we wanted to steep in the now? What if we could stay right here? Olender captures the anxieties of an ever-moving world with a sweet and silly sincerity on her second full-length album The Worrier, never shying away from staring the truth right in the face. Exploring anxiety, sexuality and anarchy, both in herself and in her relationships, Olender’s acoustic, bluesy melodies point to the butterflies of first crushes, the gut-punching realities of responsibility and the eternal romance of friendship. #alolender #fruitbats #theramkat #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: 🎵 Paul Thorn
events.description:
There'll be no tough times at The Ramkat on October 2 when we welcome paulthornmusic! 

When it comes to songwriting, less is more, and simplicity is strength. Just ask Paul Thorn, who’s spent three decades turning soulful grooves and small syllables into songs that pack a big wallop. Maybe he learned the power of minimalism from his years as a pro boxer; maybe it just comes naturally. But whether he’s targeting heads, hearts, hips or the occasional funny bone, he somehow manages to condense large nuggets of wisdom into tight little mantras, the kind embroiderers stitched onto pillows before internet memes existed.

Thorn’s new album, Life is Just A Vapor, contains some beauties: “Life is a vapor, let’s live it while we can”; “tough times don’t last, but tough people do” (from “Tough Times Don’t Last”); “any mountain up ahead is just a hill” (from “Old Melodies”). They’re words of advice, comfort, support, encouragement, often meant to uplift, especially in times of struggle.

“I like for people to be touched by music and get something from it, something that they can take with them throughout the day,” Thorn says. “Every song on this album, there's a message in it of some sort about how to live life.”

Get tickets at TheRamkat.com! 

#theramkat #paulthorn #singersongwriter #livemusic #wsnc

events.title: 🎵 Chris Knight with Special Guest Micharrison
events.description:
Pleased to welcome chrisknightmusic back to The Ramkat on October 9, 2026, with special guest micharrison4! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets go on sale at 10 AM Eastern this Friday, May 22, at TheRamkat.com! 

After 28 years as a recording artist, singer-songwriter Chris Knight remains boldly empowered to make music that always delivers the unflinching truth. 
That brutally honest, no-frills philosophy fits his Americana-fueled, backwoods-grown merger of folk, country, and rock. It’s been at the backbone of nine studio albums, beginning with 1998’s acclaimed self-titled debut and traveling through scorchers such as the one-two punch of 2001’s A Pretty Good Guy and 2003’s The Jealous Kind, two demo-styled discs (2007’s The Trailer Tapes and 2009’s Trailer II), and the recent, electric guitar-fortified opus, 2019’s Almost Daylight. #chrisknight #micharrison #theramkat #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: 🎨 Acting Through Song Masterclass
events.description:
Fresh off their Junior Theatre Festival run, look what our rising sTArs have coming up next! ta.rising.stars 

✨ An unforgettable experience is coming to Winston-Salem! ✨

🎭 Tony Award-nominated Broadway star Kerry Butler is headlining an exclusive Acting Through Song Masterclass & Rising sTArs Broadway Cabaret hosted by Theatre Alliance Rising sTArs! 🌟

Kerry Butler has graced the Broadway stage originating roles in Beetlejuice, Mean Girls, Xanadu, Little Shop of Horrors, Catch Me If You Can, Hairspray, and so many more! 🌟 From Broadway to TV, she’s done it all! 📺

🎤 In this masterclass, eight performers will work one-on-one with Kerry and all attendees will receive invaluable tips on audition techniques, character development, and vocal performance. PLUS, there will be a Q&A! 🙌

🎶 After the class, join Kerry and local performers for a one-night-only cabaret event featuring show-stopping performances and behind-the-scenes stories from the Broadway world. 🎭✨

Want to sing on stage with Kerry? 🎤 Registration for the masterclass includes a ticket to the cabaret and the chance to perform LIVE! 🎶💥

📅 Masterclass: March 8th, 2026 | 1-5 PM
📍 Location: Juxtaposition Studios, Winston-Salem, NC
🎟️ Registration Fee: $75 | Register at link in comments 

🌟 Cabaret: March 8th, 2026 | 8 PM
📍 Location: Winston-Salem Theatre Alliance
🎟️ Tickets: $25 | Get yours here:  https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Don’t miss your chance to learn from one of Broadway’s brightest stars! ✨

#KerryButler #BroadwayMasterclass #TheatreAlliance #RisingStars #WinstonSalemEvents

events.title: 🎨 This Side of Crazy
events.location: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance - 651 Pilot View St NW Winston Salem, NC 27101
events.description:
Don’t be an outsider looking in! Enjoy a matinee with us this Saturday!

“This Side of Crazy,” by Del Shores, is next in Theatre Alliance’s Beall Theatre! When three feuding sisters are summoned home by their popular gospel-music composing mother to perform one final musical concert, old tensions flare, and the outcome is anything but blessed and holy! Grab a front-row seat to the latest by the southern writer who brought us “Sordid Lives,” “Southern Baptist Sissies,” and “The Trials and Tribulations of a Trailer Trash Housewife!”

September 20 and 27 at 12 PM and 3 PM

Tickets: $15

Get them here: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Rated R for language, sexual content, mature subject matter
